params.name: Lu Ji's Calligraphy
params.description: Lu Ji is a calligrapher from the Western Jin Dynasty. It is a letter to a friend inquiring about his recovery from illness. It was written in a cursive script that transitioned from archaic cursive to modern cursive, characterized by its gentle strokes and simple, robust style. The original piece contained nine lines with approximately eighty-four characters, but only three lines and twenty-two characters remain today. It is one of the earliest surviving literati ink writings to date, holding im …
